title: MVP Epic: recoverly-sim brain injury recovery simulation (kinematics + Ji Lab CNN or custom PINN/GNN → strain → recovery trajectories)
description: Overall MVP epic for the brand new recoverly-sim: platform to go from head impact kinematics (synthetic or real), through brain strain estimation (integrate or stub Ji Lab CNNs for regional/voxel MPS), to stochastic recovery simulation of function/symptoms with modifiers and setbacks. Includes data model, CLI, exports, training scaffold for lightweight models, docs, CI, dual-remote push. All work tracked in child issues.
design: Rust-primary crate for engine+CLI (fast MC, determinism); scripts/julia/python for Ji integration + training (PINN/GNN); interchange via JSON/CSV. See plan for full model, Ji repos (GPL-3), citations. MVP keeps scope to bridge + sim engine + scaffold (no committing Ji weights).
acceptance_criteria: All child issues closed; full verification criteria from plan pass (CLI runs with synthetic/Ji, exports valid, tests green, pushes clean, README has Ji citations + owns/not-owns, CLAUDE updated).

title: Ji Lab CNN integration layer (bridge + docs + example using their pretrained; + stub for custom lightweight model)
description: Provide integration for the Ji Lab CNNs (https://github.com/Jilab-biomechanics/CNN-estimation-of-brain-strain-distribution and CNN-brain-strains). For MVP: documented bridge (subprocess to their python/matlab or modern torch reimpl), synthetic fallback, example invocation. Stub for plugging custom PINN/GNN strain predictor. Include download helper for external weights. Full citations.
design: Start with bridge for quick validation (call their predict scripts via temp .mat or direct if modernized); provide synthetic kinematics + strain generator. Later follow-up for pure Rust inference (candle/ONNX). Expose as module so recovery engine can consume StrainMetrics from it or custom.

title: Core recovery MC engine + modifiers + milestone detection (lib, seeded, testable)
description: Implement the day-by-day (or integrated) stochastic recovery simulation in Rust lib. Initial damage from strain metrics → function/symptom trajectories. Modifiers (age, adherence, etc.). Seeded RNG for determinism. Milestone extraction (e.g. days to 80% recovery). Aggregates over MC runs.

title: Setup dual git remotes (origin=GH, gitlab=GL), normalize branch to main, initial push of beads skeleton
description: Add the two remotes for the pre-created empty recoverly-sim repos on GitHub and GitLab. Rename branch from Main to main. Perform the first push so both remotes are populated with the current beads skeleton. Verify git status shows up-to-date after. This is the very first actionable item so code changes can be pushed per session rules.
design: Remotes: origin=git@github.com:rmems/recoverly-sim.git ; gitlab=git@gitlab.com:rmems/recoverly-sim.git . Use ssh per sibling convention. Branch -M main before push. Since remotes are empty, normal push will work; no force needed.
acceptance_criteria: git remote -v lists origin and gitlab correctly; git branch --show-current is main; git push -u succeeds to both; git status reports up to date with origin/main after a pull/push cycle.

title: CLI (clap): estimate-strain, simulate-recovery, batch, list-metrics, with exports to json/csv + summary tables
description: Build the binary CLI using clap. Subcommands for strain estimation (Ji or synthetic), running recovery sims with flags for params/runs/seed/export, utilities. Pretty tables via comfy-table. Write artifacts to outputs/ by default.

title: Data schemas + types: ImpactKinematics, BrainStrain (regional + distrib summaries), InjuryMetrics, RecoveryTrace, SimConfig, PatientModifiers
description: Define and implement the core Rust types (and serialization) for kinematics profiles, strain output from Ji or synthetic, derived injury damage, recovery simulation state and traces, config, and patient/modifier params. Support serde for JSON/CSV export. Make pure and documented.
